summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on fraud/security, regulation.

articles.title: Boston Fed forms payments council
articles.description: The Federal Reserve bank is gathering input from payments players, including Toast and Wex, as well as banks to help shape its policy outlook.

articles.title: Tipalti counts on future IPO
articles.description: The finance-automation software company is focused on AI and an eventual initial public stock offering, its president said.

articles.title: Judge rules against Fiserv
articles.description: The federal court decision denied the payment processor’s motion to dismiss a credit union’s lawsuit in California. It’s one in a pack brought by such financial institutions nationwide.

articles.title: BNPL groups ‘neutral’ on Illinois bill
articles.description: Legislation to regulate buy now, pay later providers in Illinois hasn’t yet drawn the same level of industry opposition as a 2025 New York law.

articles.title: Visa, Mastercard fend off fee foes
articles.description: Despite the passage of state legislation and a federal court settlement aimed at taming credit and debit card fees, the card networks and their issuers prevailed in the first half of this year.

articles.title: Nacha’s fraud rules land
articles.description: The payments organization’s new push-credit rules force receiving financial institutions to monitor for fraud more closely.

articles.title: How payment orchestration helps merchants drive business growth
articles.description: A solid payments strategy is key for expanding into new regions and meeting evolving consumer demands.

articles.title: Deluxe makes $625M acquisition
articles.description: The paper check company seeking to evolve its business is purchasing payments processor Celero to boost its non-check revenue.
